title: Providerâ†”consumer relationship graph & governance â€” ingestion, seam-contracts, and the WE/Plateau split
summary: Prior-art survey grounding decision #092 (the control plane that makes Plateau an enterprise web platform manager). Two ingestion models are mature industry patterns with a documented complementary consensus: build-time/declared (Backstage catalog-info.yaml spec.providesApis/consumesApis; CycloneDX SBOM) â€” complete and auditable but lost-at-runtime and hand-authored-drift; and runtime-agent (OpenTelemetry service-graph connector) â€” only-accurate-from-real-traffic but stale-fast. The field recommends both: static discovery validated by runtime, edges marked potential until trace-confirmed. The WE-specific reframe: WE introspectable registries/injectors/contexts/events ARE the graph, so the build-time manifest is auto-derived (no hand-authored catalog-info.yaml bottleneck) â€” making build-first staging natural. Seam-contracts map to consumer-driven contract testing (Pact broker as the safe-to-deploy-together source of truth; drift detection reactive to proactive), reused from WE protocol conformance contracts recorded bidirectionally. Home splits per the constellation: graph-model and contracts are the WE open standard, aggregation and UI and governance are the Plateau licensed product. Report: reports/2026-06-12-provider-consumer-graph.md.
